title: Statice littoralis
intro: Statice littoralis, also known as sea lavender or sea rosemary, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, white, pink, or purple flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. Statice littoralis contains a range of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Statice littoralis is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Asterales, and family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Limonium, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as sea lavenders. The species Limonium sinuatum is often confused with Statice littoralis. However, the two are distinct and can be differentiated by their physical characteristics. Statice littoralis is also known as Limonium sinuatum in some parts of the world.
uses: Statice littoralis has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is said to have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, suggesting its potential as an antimicrobial agent. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. Further studies are required to confirm the efficacy and safety of Statice littoralis for medicinal purposes.

These compounds contribute to the plant's potential health benefits and pharmacological activities. Flavonoids in Statice littoralis have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Carotenoids, specifically xanthophylls, are known for their role in protecting the plant from oxidative stress and UV radiation. parts: Statice littoralis is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, but information on its medicinal parts is limited.
